metadata.name: Digital Operational Resilience Act (DORA)
metadata.description: The Digital Operational Resilience Act (DORA) is an EU regulation (2022/2554) that establishes a comprehensive framework for digital operational resilience in the financial sector. It sets uniform requirements for the security of network and information systems supporting the business processes of financial entities, covering ICT risk management, incident reporting, operational resilience testing, third-party risk management, and information sharing.
metadata.source_organization: European Parliament and Council of the European Union
metadata.classification_reasoning: DORA is a binding EU regulation that directly applies to all EU member states without need for national transposition, imposing mandatory requirements on financial entities for digital operational resilience.
metadata.framework_purpose: To consolidate and upgrade ICT risk requirements across the EU financial sector, ensuring all participants in the financial system have the necessary safeguards to mitigate cyber-attacks and other ICT-related risks.
metadata.framework_scope: All EU financial entities including credit institutions, payment institutions, electronic money institutions, investment firms, crypto-asset service providers, insurance and reinsurance undertakings, pension funds, credit rating agencies, and ICT third-party service providers designated as critical.
framework_objectives: Establish comprehensive ICT risk management frameworks
Harmonize ICT-related incident classification and reporting
Mandate digital operational resilience testing including threat-led penetration testing
Manage ICT third-party risks through contractual and oversight frameworks
Enable voluntary information sharing on cyber threats
metadata.target_audience: Financial entities, ICT third-party service providers, competent authorities, European Supervisory Authorities (ESAs), and compliance/risk management teams in the EU financial sector
required_artifacts: ICT Incident Response and Reporting Procedures
metadata.penalty_for_non_compliance: Competent authorities may impose administrative penalties and remedial measures including periodic penalty payments, public notices, orders to cease conduct, and monetary sanctions as determined by national law implementing DORA's penalty framework.
adoption_approach: Conduct gap assessment against DORA requirements
Establish or enhance ICT risk management framework
Implement ICT incident classification and reporting capabilities
Develop digital operational resilience testing programme
Review and update all ICT third-party contracts
Document and maintain oversight framework for critical ICT providers

sections: Chapter III: ICT-Related Incident Management, Classification and Reporting (Articles 17-23)
Chapter IV: Digital Operational Resilience Testing (Articles 24-27)
Chapter V: Managing of ICT Third-Party Risk (Articles 28-44)

controls.title: ICT Risk Management Framework
controls.description: Financial entities shall have in place an internal governance and control framework that ensures effective and prudent management of ICT risk.
controls.full_text: Financial entities shall have in place an internal governance and control framework that ensures an effective and prudent management of ICT risk, in accordance with Article 6(4), in order to achieve a high level of digital operational resilience. The management body of the financial entity shall define, approve, oversee and be responsible for the implementation of all arrangements related to the ICT risk management framework.
evidence_requirements: Board approval records for ICT risk framework

controls.title: Management Body Responsibility for ICT Risk
controls.description: The management body shall bear ultimate responsibility for managing the financial entity's ICT risk and shall set clear roles and responsibilities for ICT-related functions.
controls.full_text: The management body of the financial entity shall define, approve, oversee and be responsible for the implementation of all arrangements related to the ICT risk management framework referred to in Article 6(4). The management body shall bear the ultimate responsibility for managing the financial entity's ICT risk and shall set clear roles and responsibilities for all ICT-related functions, including the definition of appropriate tolerance levels for ICT risk.
evidence_requirements: Roles and responsibilities matrix for ICT functions
Management body meeting minutes covering ICT risk

controls.title: Management Body ICT Training
controls.description: Members of the management body shall maintain sufficient knowledge and skills to understand and assess ICT risk and its impact on operations.
controls.full_text: Members of the management body of the financial entity shall actively keep up to date with sufficient knowledge and skills to understand and assess ICT risk and its impact on the operations of the financial entity, including by following specific training on a regular basis, commensurate to the ICT risk being managed.

controls.title: Sound, Comprehensive and Well-Documented ICT Risk Management Framework
controls.description: Financial entities shall have a sound, comprehensive and well-documented ICT risk management framework as part of their overall risk management system.
controls.full_text: Financial entities shall have a sound, comprehensive and well-documented ICT risk management framework as part of their overall risk management system, which enables them to address ICT risk quickly, efficiently and comprehensively and to ensure a high level of digital operational resilience.
evidence_requirements: Comprehensive ICT risk management framework document
Integration with overall risk management system

controls.title: ICT Risk Management Framework Strategies and Policies
controls.description: The ICT risk management framework shall include strategies, policies, procedures, ICT protocols and tools necessary to protect ICT assets and infrastructure.
controls.full_text: The ICT risk management framework referred to in paragraph 1 shall include at least strategies, policies, procedures, ICT protocols and tools that are necessary to duly and adequately protect all information assets and ICT assets, including computer software, hardware, servers, as well as to protect all the relevant physical components and infrastructures.

controls.title: ICT Risk Management Framework Review
controls.description: Financial entities shall review their ICT risk management framework documentation at least once a year or upon major ICT-related incidents.
controls.full_text: Financial entities, other than microenterprises, shall on a yearly basis, and upon the occurrence of major ICT-related incidents, review their ICT risk management framework and submit a report on the review to the competent authority.

controls.title: Digital Operational Resilience Strategy
controls.description: Financial entities shall define and implement a digital operational resilience strategy as an integral component of the ICT risk management framework.
controls.full_text: As part of the ICT risk management framework referred to in Article 6(1), financial entities shall implement a digital operational resilience strategy. The digital operational resilience strategy shall set out how the framework is implemented. It shall include methods to address ICT risk and attain specific ICT objectives, by setting out the key dependencies on ICT third-party service providers and explaining the overall ICT multi-vendor strategy.
evidence_requirements: ICT objectives and risk appetite alignment

controls.title: ICT Systems, Protocols and Tools Requirements
controls.description: Financial entities shall use and maintain updated ICT systems, protocols and tools that are appropriate to the scale and complexity of operations.
controls.full_text: Financial entities shall use and maintain updated ICT systems, protocols and tools that are appropriate to the scale of operations supporting their activities, in accordance with the proportionality principle as referred to in Article 4, reliable, have sufficient capacity, and are technologically resilient.
evidence_requirements: ICT systems inventory and currency assessment

controls.title: Identification of ICT-Supported Business Functions and Assets
controls.description: Financial entities shall identify, classify and adequately document all ICT-supported business functions, roles and responsibilities, information assets and ICT assets.
controls.full_text: As part of the ICT risk management framework referred to in Article 6(1), financial entities shall identify, classify and adequately document all ICT-supported business functions, roles and responsibilities, the information assets and ICT assets supporting those functions, and their roles and dependencies in relation to ICT risk.
evidence_requirements: Business function mapping to ICT assets

controls.title: ICT Risk Assessment and Documentation
controls.description: Financial entities shall identify all sources of ICT risk, assess cyber threats and ICT vulnerabilities, and review risk scenarios regularly.
controls.full_text: Financial entities shall on a continuous basis identify all sources of ICT risk, in particular the risk exposure to and from other financial entities, and assess cyber threats and ICT vulnerabilities relevant to their ICT-supported business functions, information assets and ICT assets. Financial entities shall review on a regular basis, at least yearly, the risk scenarios impacting them.

controls.title: ICT Security Protection and Prevention Policies
controls.description: Financial entities shall use ICT security tools, policies and procedures to protect all information assets and ICT assets.
controls.full_text: For the purposes of adequately protecting ICT systems and with a view to organising response measures, financial entities shall continuously monitor and control the security and functioning of ICT systems and tools and shall minimise the impact of ICT risk on ICT systems through the deployment of appropriate ICT security tools, policies and procedures.

controls.title: ICT Security Mechanisms and Controls
controls.description: Financial entities shall design and implement ICT security policies, procedures and controls covering authentication, access control, encryption, and network security.
controls.full_text: Financial entities shall design, procure and implement ICT security policies, procedures, protocols and tools that aim to ensure the resilience, continuity and availability of ICT systems, in particular for those supporting critical or important functions, and maintain high standards of availability, authenticity, integrity and confidentiality of data, whether at rest, in use or in transit.

controls.title: ICT Change Management Procedures
controls.description: Financial entities shall implement sound ICT change management procedures for all changes to ICT systems including patches, updates and configuration changes.
controls.full_text: In order to achieve the objectives referred to in paragraph 2, financial entities shall make use of ICT solutions and processes that are appropriate in accordance with Article 4. Those ICT solutions and processes shall implement an ICT change management process, including procedures for emergencies, to ensure that all changes to ICT systems are recorded, tested, assessed, approved, implemented and verified in a controlled manner.

controls.title: ICT Anomaly Detection and Response
controls.description: Financial entities shall establish mechanisms to promptly detect anomalous activities including ICT network performance issues and ICT-related incidents.
controls.full_text: Financial entities shall have in place mechanisms to promptly detect anomalous activities, in accordance with Article 17, including ICT network performance issues and ICT-related incidents, and to identify all potential material single points of failure. All detection mechanisms shall be regularly tested in accordance with Article 25.

controls.title: Multi-Layer Detection Controls
controls.description: Financial entities shall implement detection controls enabling multiple layers of control with defined alert thresholds and criteria for incident response.
controls.full_text: The detection mechanisms referred to in paragraph 1 shall enable multiple layers of control, define alert thresholds and criteria to trigger and initiate ICT-related incident response processes, including automatic alert mechanisms for staff in charge of ICT-related incident response.

controls.title: ICT Business Continuity Policy
controls.description: Financial entities shall put in place a comprehensive ICT business continuity policy as an integral part of the overall business continuity policy.
controls.full_text: As part of the ICT risk management framework referred to in Article 6(1), financial entities shall put in place a comprehensive ICT business continuity policy, which may be adopted as a dedicated specific policy, forming an integral part of the overall business continuity policy of the financial entity.

controls.title: Business Impact Analysis of ICT Disruptions
controls.description: Financial entities shall conduct a business impact analysis of their exposure to severe business disruptions considering ICT risk.
controls.full_text: Financial entities shall implement the ICT business continuity policy through dedicated, appropriate and documented arrangements, plans, procedures and mechanisms aimed at ensuring the continuity of the financial entity's critical or important functions, quickly, appropriately and effectively responding to and resolving all ICT-related incidents, and activating without delay dedicated plans enabling containment measures, processes and technologies.

controls.title: ICT Business Continuity Plans
controls.description: Financial entities shall implement ICT business continuity plans that cover all critical functions, are tested regularly, and are updated based on testing outcomes.
controls.full_text: Financial entities shall implement ICT business continuity plans that shall be subject to regular testing, at least annually, including with respect to scenarios of severe business disruption. The plans shall be reviewed in the light of the results of tests, audits and supervisory reviews.

controls.title: ICT Disaster Recovery Plans
controls.description: Financial entities shall establish ICT response and recovery plans subject to independent audit reviews.
controls.full_text: Financial entities shall put in place, maintain and periodically test appropriate ICT response and recovery plans, which, for financial entities other than microenterprises, shall be subject to independent audit reviews. Financial entities shall regularly test the plans and consider scenarios of cyber-attacks and switchovers between the primary ICT infrastructure and the redundant capacity.

controls.title: Crisis Communication Plans
controls.description: Financial entities shall put in place crisis communication plans enabling responsible disclosure of major ICT-related incidents to stakeholders.
controls.full_text: Financial entities shall put in place crisis communication plans enabling a responsible disclosure of, at least, major ICT-related incidents or vulnerabilities to clients and counterparts as well as to the public, as appropriate.

controls.title: Backup Policies and Procedures
controls.description: Financial entities shall establish backup policies specifying scope, frequency, and methods for backing up data and ICT systems.
controls.full_text: For the purpose of ensuring the restoration of ICT systems and data with minimum downtime, limited disruption and loss, as part of their ICT risk management framework, financial entities shall develop and document backup policies and procedures specifying the scope of data and ICT systems subject to backup, the frequency of backup, and backup and restoration methods.

controls.title: Backup System Separation
controls.description: Backup systems shall be physically and logically segregated from the source system to prevent compromise in case of attacks.
controls.full_text: When restoring backup data using own systems, financial entities shall use ICT systems that are physically and logically segregated from the source ICT system. The ICT systems shall be securely protected from any unauthorised access or ICT corruption and shall allow for the timely restoration of services making use of data and system backups as necessary.

controls.title: ICT Lessons Learned and Post-Incident Reviews
controls.description: Financial entities shall develop capabilities and staffing to gather information on vulnerabilities, threats, and ICT-related incidents, and conduct post-incident reviews.
controls.full_text: Financial entities shall have in place capabilities and staff to gather information on vulnerabilities and cyber threats, ICT-related incidents, in particular cyber-attacks, and analyse the impact they are likely to have on their digital operational resilience. Financial entities shall put in place post ICT-related incident reviews after a major ICT-related incident disrupts their core activities.

controls.title: ICT Security Awareness and Training
controls.description: Financial entities shall develop ICT security awareness programmes and digital operational resilience training as part of compulsory training for all staff.
controls.full_text: Financial entities shall develop ICT security awareness programmes and digital operational resilience training as compulsory modules in their staff training schemes. Those programmes and training shall be applicable to all employees and to senior management staff, and shall have a level of complexity commensurate to the functions of their remit.

controls.title: ICT Risk Management for Simplified Framework
controls.description: Financial entities that qualify as microenterprises or are subject to the simplified ICT risk management framework shall establish and maintain a sound and documented ICT risk management framework proportionate to their size.
controls.full_text: Financial entities referred to in Article 16(1) shall establish and maintain a sound and documented ICT risk management framework that details the mechanisms and measures aimed at a quick, efficient and comprehensive management of ICT risk, including for the protection of relevant physical components and infrastructures.

controls.title: ICT-Related Incident Management Process
controls.description: Financial entities shall define, establish and implement an ICT-related incident management process to detect, manage and notify ICT-related incidents.
controls.full_text: Financial entities shall define, establish and implement an ICT-related incident management process to detect, manage and notify ICT-related incidents. Financial entities shall record all ICT-related incidents and significant cyber threats. Financial entities shall establish appropriate procedures and processes to ensure a consistent and integrated monitoring, handling and follow-up of ICT-related incidents.

controls.title: Early Warning Indicators for ICT Incidents
controls.description: Financial entities shall establish early warning indicators to enable early detection and proactive handling of ICT-related incidents.
controls.full_text: Financial entities shall establish appropriate procedures and processes to ensure a consistent and integrated monitoring, handling and follow-up of ICT-related incidents, to ensure that root causes are identified, documented and addressed in order to prevent the occurrence of such incidents. The incident management process shall put in place early warning indicators as alerts.

controls.title: ICT-Related Incident Classification
controls.description: Financial entities shall classify ICT-related incidents and determine their impact based on specified criteria including number of clients, duration, geographical spread, and data losses.
controls.full_text: Financial entities shall classify ICT-related incidents and shall determine their impact on the basis of the following criteria: (a) the number and/or relevance of clients or financial counterparts affected; (b) the duration of the ICT-related incident; (c) the geographical spread; (d) the data losses; (e) the criticality of the services affected; (f) the economic impact.

controls.title: Major ICT-Related Incident Reporting
controls.description: Financial entities shall report major ICT-related incidents to the relevant competent authority using standardized templates and within specified timeframes.
controls.full_text: Financial entities shall report major ICT-related incidents to the relevant competent authority as referred to in Article 46 in accordance with paragraph 4 of this Article. Where a financial entity is subject to supervision by more than one national competent authority, Member States shall designate a single competent authority as the relevant competent authority for the purposes of this Article.

controls.title: Incident Reporting Timelines
controls.description: Financial entities shall submit initial notification, intermediate report and final report for major ICT-related incidents within prescribed timeframes.
controls.full_text: Financial entities shall, within the time limits to be set in accordance with Article 20, point (a)(ii), submit to the relevant competent authority: (a) an initial notification; (b) an intermediate report after the initial notification, as soon as the status of the original incident has changed significantly or the handling of the major ICT-related incident has changed; (c) a final report, when the root cause analysis has been completed.
evidence_requirements: Final report template with root cause analysis

controls.title: Client Notification of Major ICT Incidents
controls.description: Financial entities shall inform their clients without undue delay about major ICT-related incidents that may adversely affect their financial interests.
controls.full_text: Financial entities shall, without undue delay, inform their clients about the major ICT-related incident and about the measures that have been taken to mitigate the adverse effects of such incident. In the case of a significant cyber threat, financial entities shall, where applicable, inform their clients that are potentially affected of any appropriate protection measures which the latter may consider taking.

controls.title: Significant Cyber Threat Voluntary Reporting
controls.description: Financial entities may, on a voluntary basis, notify the relevant competent authority of significant cyber threats they identify.
controls.full_text: Financial entities may, on a voluntary basis, notify the relevant competent authority of significant cyber threats that they deem to be of relevance to the financial system, service users or clients. The relevant competent authority may provide that information to other relevant public authorities.

controls.title: General Requirements for Digital Operational Resilience Testing
controls.description: Financial entities shall establish a sound and comprehensive digital operational resilience testing programme as an integral part of the ICT risk management framework.
controls.full_text: For the purpose of assessing preparedness for handling ICT-related incidents, of identifying weaknesses, deficiencies and gaps in digital operational resilience, and of promptly implementing corrective measures, financial entities, other than microenterprises, shall, taking into account the criteria set out in Article 4(2), establish, maintain and review a sound and comprehensive digital operational resilience testing programme as an integral part of the ICT risk management framework.
evidence_requirements: Digital operational resilience testing programme documentation
Testing results and corrective actions tracking

controls.title: Risk-Based Testing Approach
controls.description: The digital operational resilience testing programme shall include a range of assessments, tests, methodologies, practices and tools.
controls.full_text: The digital operational resilience testing programme referred to in paragraph 1 shall include a range of assessments, tests, methodologies, practices and tools to be applied in accordance with Articles 25 and 26, following a risk-based approach and having due regard to the evolving landscape of ICT risk.

controls.title: ICT Tools and Systems Testing
controls.description: Financial entities shall carry out appropriate tests including vulnerability assessments and scans, open source analyses, network security assessments, gap analyses, and scenario-based tests.
controls.full_text: The digital operational resilience testing programme referred to in Article 24 shall provide for the execution of appropriate tests, such as vulnerability assessments and scans, open source analyses, network security assessments, gap analyses, physical security reviews, questionnaires and scanning software solutions, source code reviews where feasible, scenario-based tests, compatibility testing, performance testing, end-to-end testing and penetration testing.

controls.title: Annual Testing of Critical ICT Systems
controls.description: Financial entities shall test all critical ICT systems and applications at least yearly.
controls.full_text: Financial entities, other than microenterprises, shall test all ICT systems and applications supporting critical or important functions at least yearly.
evidence_requirements: Annual testing plan for critical systems

controls.title: Threat-Led Penetration Testing (TLPT)
controls.description: Financial entities identified by competent authorities shall carry out threat-led penetration testing (TLPT) at least every three years.
controls.full_text: Financial entities, identified in accordance with paragraph 8, third and fourth subparagraphs, shall carry out at least every 3 years advanced testing by means of TLPT. Based on the risk profile of the financial entity and taking into account operational circumstances, the competent authority may, where necessary, request the financial entity to reduce or increase that frequency.
evidence_requirements: TLPT execution reports and remediation plans

controls.title: TLPT Scope and Live Production Systems
controls.description: Threat-led penetration testing shall cover critical or important functions and be performed on live production systems.
controls.full_text: The threat-led penetration test shall cover several or all critical or important functions of a financial entity, and shall be performed on live production systems supporting such functions. Financial entities shall identify all relevant underlying ICT systems, processes and technologies supporting critical or important functions, including those supporting functions outsourced or contracted to ICT third-party service providers.
evidence_requirements: TLPT scope documentation covering critical functions

controls.title: TLPT Tester Requirements
controls.description: Threat-led penetration tests shall be performed by external testers meeting specific requirements regarding reputation, technical capability, and certification.
controls.full_text: Financial entities shall engage testers for the purposes of undertaking TLPT in accordance with Article 27. When financial entities use internal testers for the purposes of undertaking TLPT, they shall engage external testers every three tests. ICT third-party service providers providing critical or important functions shall participate in the TLPT as relevant.

controls.title: ICT Third-Party Risk General Principles
controls.description: Financial entities shall manage ICT third-party risk as an integral component of ICT risk within their ICT risk management framework.
controls.full_text: Financial entities shall manage ICT third-party risk as an integral component of ICT risk within their ICT risk management framework as referred to in Article 6(1), and in accordance with the following principles: (a) financial entities that have in place contractual arrangements for the use of ICT services shall at all times remain fully responsible for compliance with, and discharge of, all obligations under this Regulation and applicable financial services law.
evidence_requirements: Integration with ICT risk framework documentation

controls.title: Strategy on ICT Third-Party Risk
controls.description: Financial entities shall adopt and regularly review a strategy on ICT third-party risk taking into account the multi-vendor strategy.
controls.full_text: Financial entities shall, on a proportionate basis, having regard to their size and overall risk profile, and the nature, scale and complexity of their services, activities and operations, adopt, and regularly review, a strategy on ICT third-party risk, including a policy on the use of ICT services supporting critical or important functions provided by ICT third-party service providers on an individual and on a consolidated basis.

controls.title: Register of ICT Third-Party Arrangements
controls.description: Financial entities shall maintain and update a register of information in relation to all contractual arrangements on the use of ICT services provided by third-party service providers.
controls.full_text: As part of their ICT risk management framework, financial entities shall maintain and update at entity level, and at sub-consolidated and consolidated levels, a register of information in relation to all contractual arrangements on the use of ICT services provided by ICT third-party service providers. The contractual arrangements shall be appropriately documented, distinguishing between those that cover critical or important functions and those that do not.

controls.title: Annual Reporting on ICT Third-Party Arrangements
controls.description: Financial entities shall report at least annually to the competent authorities on the number of new arrangements, categories of ICT third-party service providers, and type of contractual arrangements.
controls.full_text: Financial entities shall at least annually, report to the competent authorities on the number of new arrangements on the use of ICT services, the categories of ICT third-party service providers, the type of contractual arrangements and the ICT services and functions which are being provided.

controls.title: ICT Concentration Risk Assessment
controls.description: Financial entities shall identify and assess whether conclusion of contractual arrangements would lead to ICT concentration risk.
controls.full_text: Financial entities shall, prior to concluding a contractual arrangement on the use of ICT services, identify and assess all relevant risks in relation to the contractual arrangement, including the possibility that such contractual arrangement may contribute to reinforcing ICT concentration risk as referred to in Article 29.

controls.title: Preliminary Assessment of ICT Third-Party Providers
controls.description: Financial entities shall conduct a preliminary assessment of ICT concentration risk, due diligence, and fitness assessment before entering into contractual arrangements.
controls.full_text: When assessing whether the contractual arrangement with ICT third-party service providers on the use of ICT services supporting critical or important functions is adequate, financial entities shall carry out due diligence and throughout the selection and assessment process, ensure that the ICT third-party service provider is suitable, including by assessing whether the concentration of ICT services with a limited number of ICT third-party service providers may increase the financial entity's vulnerability.

controls.title: Key Contractual Provisions for ICT Services
controls.description: Contractual arrangements for ICT services shall include clear descriptions of services, data processing locations, service levels, and termination provisions.
controls.full_text: The contractual arrangements on the use of ICT services shall include at least the following elements: (a) a clear and complete description of all functions and ICT services to be provided; (b) the locations where the contracted or subcontracted functions and ICT services are to be provided and where data is to be processed; (c) provisions on availability, authenticity, integrity and confidentiality in relation to data protection; (d) provisions on ensuring access, recovery and return of data in a comprehensible format.
evidence_requirements: Contract review checklists covering required provisions

controls.title: Contractual Provisions for Critical Functions
controls.description: Contractual arrangements for critical or important functions shall include additional requirements including guaranteed service levels, notice periods, and exit strategies.
controls.full_text: When concluding contractual arrangements on the use of ICT services supporting critical or important functions, financial entities shall ensure that the contractual arrangements include additionally: (a) full service level descriptions with precise quantitative and qualitative performance targets; (b) relevant notice periods and reporting obligations of the ICT third-party service provider; (c) exit strategies and dedicated transition plans.
evidence_requirements: Enhanced contract provisions for critical functions

controls.title: Right of Access, Inspection and Audit
controls.description: Financial entities shall ensure contractual arrangements include unrestricted rights of access, inspection and audit by the financial entity or appointed third party.
controls.full_text: The contractual arrangements on the use of ICT services supporting critical or important functions shall also include provisions establishing unrestricted rights of access, inspection and audit for the financial entity, or an appointed third party, with right of access to all relevant premises, information, and systems and processes of the ICT third-party service provider.

controls.title: Designation of Critical ICT Third-Party Service Providers
controls.description: The ESAs shall designate ICT third-party service providers that are critical for financial entities through the Joint Committee, applying specified criteria.
controls.full_text: The ESAs, through the Joint Committee, shall designate the ICT third-party service providers that are critical for financial entities, after an assessment that takes into account: (a) the systemic impact on the stability, continuity or quality of the provision of financial services; (b) the systemic character or importance of the financial entities that rely on the critical ICT third-party service provider; (c) the reliance of financial entities on the services provided; (d) the degree of substitutability.

controls.title: Lead Overseer Powers and Responsibilities
controls.description: The Lead Overseer shall assess whether critical ICT third-party service providers have comprehensive, sound and effective rules, procedures, mechanisms and arrangements to manage ICT risk.
controls.full_text: The Lead Overseer shall assess whether each critical ICT third-party service provider has in place comprehensive, sound and effective rules, procedures, mechanisms and arrangements to manage the ICT risk which it may pose to financial entities. That assessment shall focus in particular on ICT services provided by the critical ICT third-party service provider supporting the critical or important functions of financial entities.
evidence_requirements: Critical provider ICT risk management assessment
Service support mapping for critical functions

controls.title: Lead Overseer Investigation Powers
controls.description: The Lead Overseer shall have powers to request information, conduct general investigations and inspections of critical ICT third-party service providers.
controls.full_text: For the purposes of carrying out its duties under this Section, the Lead Overseer shall have the following powers in respect of critical ICT third-party service providers: (a) to request all relevant information and documentation; (b) to conduct general investigations and inspections; (c) to request reports after the completion of the investigations and inspections; (d) to issue recommendations.

controls.title: Information Sharing Arrangements
controls.description: Financial entities may exchange amongst themselves cyber threat information and intelligence, including indicators of compromise, tactics, techniques and procedures.
controls.full_text: Financial entities may exchange amongst themselves cyber threat information and intelligence, including indicators of compromise, tactics, techniques and procedures, cyber security alerts and configuration tools, to the extent that such information and intelligence sharing: (a) aims to enhance the digital operational resilience of financial entities; (b) takes place within trusted communities of financial entities; (c) is implemented through information-sharing arrangements that protect the potentially sensitive nature of the information shared.
evidence_requirements: Data protection measures for shared information

controls.title: Information Sharing Governance
controls.description: Information sharing arrangements shall define conditions for participation, involvement of public authorities, and operational elements including use of dedicated IT platforms.
controls.full_text: The information-sharing arrangements referred to in paragraph 1 shall set out the conditions for participation and, where appropriate, the details on the involvement of public authorities and the capacity in which they may be associated to the information-sharing arrangements, on the involvement of ICT third-party service providers, and on the operational elements, including the use of dedicated IT platforms.

controls.title: Notification of Information Sharing Participation
controls.description: Financial entities shall notify competent authorities of their participation in information sharing arrangements.
controls.full_text: Financial entities shall notify competent authorities of their participation in the information-sharing arrangements referred to in paragraph 1, upon validation of their membership, and, subsequently, of any changes to that membership, or shall notify their withdrawal from such arrangements, as applicable.
